automotive-ev-battery.title: 2026 Kia CEO Investor Day: Kia to Drive Exponential Growth & Manufacturing Innovation by Expanding Its Full Electrified Vehicle Lineup and Strengthening Future Business Capabilities
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia presents its 2030 mid- to long-term strategy targeting global sales of 4.13 million units per year by 2030 with a 4.5% global market share goal. The company plans to expand its EV lineup to 14 models as part of its comprehensive electrification strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Expanding Across Canada With 20 Locations
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD announced plans to launch 20 dealerships across Canada this year, with three locations already under discussion in Toronto. Additional dealerships are planned for Montreal, Vancouver, and Calgary, marking a significant expansion for the Chinese EV manufacturer in the North American market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla reportedly adds Sunwoda as additional battery cell supplier
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer Sunwoda has reportedly been selected as Tesla's fifth battery cell supplier for its electric vehicles. The cells are allegedly already being used in vehicles manufactured at Tesla's Shanghai plant for export markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Partial permit for Heide plant as Lyten/Northvolt repays millions
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Northvolt Drei Projektgesellschaft has secured key partial approval for its battery cell factory construction in Heide, Germany, as part of the planned acquisition by US battery manufacturer Lyten. The company has also repaid €153 million from a convertible bond to federal and state governments.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla reportedly plans smaller, more affordable SUV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla is reportedly evaluating a smaller electric SUV positioned below the Model Y to target a lower price segment. It remains unclear whether the project has received approval amid uncertainty over shifting priorities under CEO Elon Musk.

automotive-ev-battery.title: What Tesla's Supercharger costs for business customers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has launched an online configurator revealing pricing for its Supercharger stations sold to corporate customers in the US. A charging park with eight charging points, including installation, costs approximately $940,000 on average.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GM faces lawsuit over Cadillac Lyriq EV defects
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors is facing a class action lawsuit in the United States over alleged defects in the Cadillac Lyriq. Plaintiffs claim issues with software and electrical systems can render the vehicle inoperable, while GM has declined to comment on the case.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai Motor Company Launches IONIQ Lineup Brand in China with the Premiere of Two New Concept Cars
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai Motor officially launched its all-electric IONIQ lineup brand in China, signaling renewed commitment to the NEV market. Two new design concepts, the VENUS Concept sedan and EARTH Concept family SUV, preview the future direction of the IONIQ lineup ahead of Auto China 2026.

automotive-ev-battery.title: UK: Volkswagen gives ID.4 and ID.5 larger batteries and V2L
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has introduced technical updates for the ID.4 and ID.5 in the UK, including higher battery capacities and improved efficiency. The changes also bring vehicle-to-load capability, longer ranges, and enhanced equipment across multiple trims.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Canada invests $10.6 million in EV charging infrastructure
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Government of Canada has announced $10.6 million in funding to support 14 electric vehicle charging infrastructure projects across the country. Additional funding will expand EV education through a cross-country test drive initiative.

tech-ai.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's house
tech-ai.summary: San Francisco police arrested a 20-year-old suspect who allegedly threw a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning. The incident was captured on surveillance cameras, and later that day someone matching the suspect's description made threats outside OpenAI's Mission Bay offices where he was arrested.

tech-ai.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser's delusions and ignored her warnings
tech-ai.summary: A new lawsuit alleges that OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous, including its own mass-casualty flag, while he used the AI to stalk and harass his ex-girlfriend. The case raises serious questions about AI safety measures and OpenAI's response to reported misuse of their platform.

tech-ai.title: France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
tech-ai.summary: France is making a strategic move to replace Windows with Linux across government systems as part of broader efforts to reduce dependence on American tech giants. This represents a significant shift in digital sovereignty policy that could influence other nations' technology choices.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic temporarily restricted OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude AI following pricing changes that affected OpenClaw users last week. The ban highlights ongoing tensions between AI providers and third-party developers building tools on top of AI platforms.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft finally lets Windows 11 testers unlock experimental features without ViVeTool
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is streamlining the Windows Insider Program by allowing testers to access experimental features without third-party tools like ViVeTool. The company is consolidating Dev and Canary testing rings into a new Experimental Channel alongside a refreshed Beta Channel to make the program less confusing.

tech-ai.title: Amazon Luna axes third-party game purchases
tech-ai.summary: Amazon Luna announced it will no longer support third-party game purchases and subscriptions, with previously purchased games being removed on June 10th, 2026. The cloud gaming service is discontinuing support for EA, Ubisoft, and GOG stores, though purchased games will remain playable on other platforms through users' original accounts.

tech-ai.title: Congress can finally close a mass surveillance loophole — but will they?
tech-ai.summary: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), a warrantless wiretapping authority, is set to expire on April 20th and is up for renewal in Congress. A bipartisan coalition is pushing for reforms to address surveillance concerns, while other powerful figures in both parties want a clean reauthorization without changes.

tech-ai.title: Now the FAA says gamers are the answer to its air traffic controller shortage
tech-ai.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration is launching a recruiting campaign targeting gamers to address its air traffic controller shortage, as the number of controllers has declined by 6 percent over the last decade. The Trump administration is rolling out this campaign ahead of the annual air traffic control hiring window opening on April 17th.

tech-ai.title: Little Snitch's software counter surveillance jumps from Mac to Linux
tech-ai.summary: The popular macOS network-monitoring app Little Snitch has launched on Linux, offering the same functionality for viewing and disabling unwanted connections. Early results show Ubuntu systems making far fewer internet connections than macOS, with only 9 system processes connecting over a week compared to over 100 on macOS.

tech-ai.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
tech-ai.summary: Fusion startups have collectively raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of funding concentrated among a handful of companies. This analysis highlights the significant investment flowing into fusion energy technology as companies work toward commercializing clean fusion power.

world-news.title: US-Iran talks set to bring together Vance and Iranian officials in Pakistan as ceasefire strains
world-news.summary: Vice President JD Vance is leading a U.S. delegation to Pakistan for high-level talks with Iranian representatives, marking the first such meeting since a war began over a month ago. The talks will test whether the two nations can shore up a fragile ceasefire and establish a path forward in their relationship.

world-news.title: Unsettled and uncertain: What the Iran war means around the world as US and Iran enter talks
world-news.summary: The recent war in Iran has left the Middle East unsettled with strained alliances and uncertain shifts in global economic and military power. Iran's theocracy remains intact but damaged, while gaining new economic leverage as the U.S. and Iran prepare for diplomatic engagement.

world-news.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán senses victory
world-news.summary: Péter Magyar's opposition movement is leading in polls against Hungary's long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. Tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters filled Heroes' Square in Budapest as the election approaches, potentially ending Orbán's authoritarian rule.

world-news.title: Trump likes to back winners in foreign elections. The upcoming vote in Hungary will test his clout
world-news.summary: President Trump and his administration have actively promoted Hungary's far-right Prime Minister Viktor Orbán through social media and an election-eve trip to Budapest. The Hungarian election will test Trump's influence in foreign electoral politics as part of his broader pattern of backing preferred candidates internationally.

world-news.title: Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
world-news.summary: Russian drone strikes killed at least two people and wounded two others in the Ukrainian Black Sea port city of Odesa overnight. The attacks came ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter, highlighting continued escalation in the ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Djibouti President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh is reelected for a sixth term
world-news.summary: Djibouti President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh won reelection for a sixth term with 97.81% of the vote in Friday's election. The 78-year-old has ruled the strategic Horn of Africa nation of about 1 million people for more than two decades.

world-news.title: Allies yank support for Swalwell's California governor run after sexual assault allegations
world-news.summary: Prominent supporters withdrew their backing from Rep. Eric Swalwell's California gubernatorial campaign after he denied allegations of sexually assaulting a woman twice, including when she worked for him. The congressman, who was a leading Democratic candidate, now faces calls to drop out of the race.

world-news.title: What to know about Melania Trump's statement denying knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein's crimes
world-news.summary: First Lady Melania Trump made an unexpected public statement Thursday denying any affiliation with disgraced sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein, though the timing and motivation remain unclear. Her comments have placed her at odds with the administration, which wants to end the Epstein investigation, and thrust the case back into the spotlight.

world-news.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he set up in Africa to honor his mother Princess Diana
world-news.summary: Sentebale, a charity co-founded by Prince Harry to honor Princess Diana, has filed a defamation lawsuit against him after he stepped down as patron last year. The legal action represents a significant falling out between the prince and the African charity he helped establish.

world-news.title: The myrrh tree that's key to luxury perfumes and African incomes is threatened by drought
world-news.summary: Myrrh trees in the Horn of Africa, which produce a critical ingredient for world-renowned perfumes, are under severe pressure from drought and climate change. The threat to these trees endangers both the global luxury perfume industry and the livelihoods of African communities dependent on myrrh harvesting.
